meta_title: For Freelancers: API integration guide to Solve no design skills (Intermediate Case Study)
meta_description: Learn how to API integration guide and overcome no design skills. A Intermediate-level Case Study using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: Freelancers: API Integration Guide for Non-Designers
content.seo_description: Master API integration to overcome lack of design skills. A high-impact case study for freelancers using Antigravity and Claude Code to ship professional SaaS.
content.answer_block: Overcome the lack of design skills by decoupling your UI from your logic. Use Antigravity to scaffold production-ready frontend components and Claude Code to automate API orchestration. This strategy allows you to build sophisticated, data-driven interfaces without needing a single pixel of custom design work, ensuring rapid client delivery.
content.intro:
The clock is ticking. Your client needs a high-fidelity SaaS dashboard by Friday, but your design skills are stuck in the 'wireframe' phase. You are staring at a blank screen, paralyzed by the gap between your backend logic and a professional-grade interface. This is the common bottleneck that kills freelance velocity.

But what if you didn't need to design? By leveraging API-first architectures and AI-driven scaffolding, you can bypass the design phase entirely. This guide transforms you from a stressed developer into a high-velocity architect, using Antigravity and Claude Code to build systems that look like they cost six figures, all while you focus on the logic that actually pays the bills.

steps.title: Define the Data Contract
steps.description: Before touching code, define your API schema. This ensures your frontend doesn't need to 'guess' the structure, allowing Antigravity to generate accurate type-safe interfaces.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a TypeScript interface and a mock JSON response for a SaaS dashboard user profile and analytics summary based on a RESTful API schema.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Create a schema.ts file defining the API response types for the dashboard based on the provided JSON spec.'

steps.title: Scaffold UI Components
steps.description: Use Antigravity to generate clean, framework-agnostic UI components that strictly adhere to your data contract, eliminating the need for custom CSS.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a responsive React component library for a dashboard using Tailwind CSS. Include a data table, summary cards, and a navigation sidebar.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Generate the UI components in /src/components using the types defined in schema.ts'

steps.title: Automate API Orchestration
steps.description: Use Claude Code to write the data-fetching layer, connecting your UI to the API without manual boilerplate.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a custom React hook 'useApiData' that handles loading states, error boundaries, and data caching for the dashboard endpoints.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Implement the useApiData hook and integrate it into the dashboard view component.'

steps.title: Inject State Management
steps.description: Connect the API responses to your UI state, ensuring that the interface updates reactively without manual DOM manipulation.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Configure a lightweight store using Zustand to manage global UI state and API data persistence for the dashboard.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Setup a store.ts file and wrap the application with the Zustand provider.'

steps.title: Final Integration and Polish
steps.description: Run a final audit to ensure data flow and layout consistency, then deploy to your staging environment.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Perform a code review on the generated dashboard components to ensure accessibility and performance best practices.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Run linter, fix any type errors, and optimize the build for production deployment.'

pro_tips: Always use a 'Design System' library like Shadcn/UI as your base; it makes AI-generated components look instantly professional.
Force your AI to output 'semantic HTML'—it improves accessibility and makes your code much easier to debug later.
Use 'Contract Testing' to ensure your frontend breaks immediately if the API response changes, saving hours of visual debugging.
Cache your API responses in localStorage during development to prevent hitting rate limits while testing UI states.
When using Claude Code, always provide the 'context' file first; it significantly reduces hallucinations in generated code.
content.next_steps: Once the dashboard is functional, implement automated end-to-end testing with Playwright to ensure your API integration remains stable as you add new features.
content.conversion_block: Stop fighting with CSS and start shipping high-value features that your clients will pay a premium for. By mastering these tools, you reclaim your time and scale your freelance business effortlessly. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
